action: Render executable and read-only screens while independently failing wallet, RPC, indexer, and analytics or price sources.

expectedAssertions.statement: Each failure names its authority rather than showing a generic or false-success state.
Only actions dependent on the failed authority are disabled.
Previously verified raw facts remain visible with freshness and partial-state disclosure.
Restoring one authority cannot revive obsolete execution context.

negativePaths.trigger: RPC identity fails while indexer and analytics respond.
USD pricing fails while RPC and complete indexer data remain trustworthy.

prerequisites: Source failures can be injected independently.
recovery: Rebuild executable context after required authorities become fresh.
title: Source failures disable only dependent actions

action: Submit a tracked write, orphan its receipt through rollback, replay it, and exercise absence through the configured timeout.

expectedAssertions.statement: Rollback removes orphaned indexed events and derived accounting without duplicates.
A formerly canonical receipt becomes reconciling or orphaned and is never silently erased.
Replay or re-inclusion restores exactly one canonical event and transaction state.
A transaction absent through timeout becomes explicitly absent or timed out, not successful.

negativePaths.trigger: The receipt block is rolled back after observation.
No original or replacement transaction is found before timeout.

prerequisites: A controllable chain can rollback and replay blocks.
RPC, indexer, analytics, and journal can be inspected.
recovery: Reconcile until re-inclusion or absence timeout.
Refresh derived state from the canonical chain.
title: Reorg rollback and replay preserve transaction truth

action: For every write class, exercise submission, reload, replacement, confirmation delay, indexer lag, reorg, revert, and possible broadcast without a returned hash.

expectedAssertions.statement: Each write persists immutable intent and submitted or replacement hashes across reload and confirmations.
Canonical success may remain downstream-reconciling, while revert and orphan states never become success.
Possible broadcast without hash becomes unknown submission and blocks retry during nonce reconciliation.
Replacement hashes stay linked to the original intent and actual nonce without duplicate logical transactions.

negativePaths.trigger: Transport fails after possible broadcast but before hash return.
The hash is replaced, reverted, orphaned, or delayed.

prerequisites: Every supported write can create a journal intent.
Nonce, txpool, receipt, and canonical blocks can be queried.
recovery: Reconcile nonce and transactions before retry.
Resume persisted intent and follow replacements to a terminal state.
title: All writes persist and reconcile ambiguous submission

action: Build an executable write, mutate each bound context field before wallet handoff, then edit a draft after handoff.

expectedAssertions.statement: The fingerprint binds account, chain, environment, contracts, verified pair identity, amounts, settings, recipients, refund, native policy, value, and supplied nonce.
Exact calldata and value are simulated against current RPC-verified factory and pair state immediately before handoff.
Any pre-handoff mutation invalidates preflight and produces no wallet request until refreshed.
After handoff the original fingerprint, prompt, hash, and nonce remain immutable while edits create a separate draft.

negativePaths.trigger: A bound execution field changes after review but before handoff.
The user edits the draft after wallet handoff.

prerequisites: A valid write can reach preflight.
recovery: Refresh identity, balances, allowances, quote, simulation, and review as a new generation.
title: Executable intents bind and simulate complete context

action: For swap, add, remove, and create entry points, compare indexed pool identity and hook metadata with configured-factory lookup and live pair factory, token order, bin step, hook flags, hook address, and hook code before simulation.
actor: Connected trader or liquidity provider on the configured chain

expectedAssertions.statement: Configured-factory lookup, live pair identity, deployment chain, and indexed pool identity agree before a write becomes executable.
Hookless and allowlisted-hook pools are distinguished using matching indexed parameters and live RPC hook address, flags, and code identity.
A foreign factory, stale or spoofed pair address, token or bin-step mismatch, unknown hook, indexer-to-RPC hook mismatch, or changed hook code produces zero simulation and wallet calls.
When execution fails closed, verified read-only pool facts remain visible with an actionable identity or hook explanation.

negativePaths.trigger: Indexer supplies a foreign, stale, or spoofed pair whose live factory, tokens, bin step, address, or chain differs from the configured deployment.
A nonzero hook is unknown or its indexed address, flags, or code identity differs from live RPC state.

prerequisites: Configured-factory, canonical pair, foreign-pair, identity-mismatch, hookless, allowlisted-hook, unknown-hook, and changed-code fixtures are available.
Simulation and wallet calls can be counted independently for each write entry point.
recovery: Refresh configured manifest, indexer, factory lookup, live pair identity, and hook code state.
Enable a new write review only after every identity field reconciles and any nonzero hook matches an explicitly supported identity; otherwise continue read-only.
title: RPC-attested pair and hook identity fail closed
